agent_approach_summary: The agent mostly pursued an unrelated worksearch/title-vs-subtitle mapping issue in `works.py`, then only late in the run started adding `luqum_replace_child` in `query_utils.py`. Even then it introduced the helper with the wrong argument order and never finished the end-to-end change.
reasoning: This task looks clean. The problem statement explicitly asks for a helper to replace a child node in a Luqum parse tree, specifies the call pattern as `(parent, old_child, new_child)`, says it should rebuild the parent’s children sequence, and says unsupported parent types should raise `ValueError`. The F2P tests do exactly that: they parse a query, traverse to a target node, call `luqum_replace_child(parents[-1], node, luqum_parser(replace_with))`, and assert the resulting serialized tree. That is not hidden or implementation-locked; it is the stated API and behavior. The gold patch contains extra `works.py` changes about `alternative_title` edition-field conversion, but the tests do not check those changes, so that is overpatch noise rather than contamination. The agent failed because it misread the task and chased that unrelated title/subtitle issue for most of the trajectory. When it finally added `luqum_replace_child`, it used the wrong signature `(old_child, new_child, parents)` instead of the stated/tested `(parent, old_child, new_child)`, and no final patch was submitted. So the failure is due to agent misunderstanding/error, not unfair tests.
